summary: Full backend + frontend regression for Quoteforge landing app. Backend pytest suite (11/11 passed) covers health, AI quote generation via gpt-5.2 (emergentintegrations), Pydantic validation (6 invalid payloads -> 422), MongoDB persistence for quotes + waitlist, and waitlist duplicate-email idempotency. Frontend Playwright run on the public preview URL confirms landing renders, estimator submits a real AI call (~10-15s) and renders quote-readout with line items + totals, waitlist form posts and shows success toast, FAQ accordion expands, header nav anchor links scroll (URL updates to #pricing), and mobile viewport shows hamburger menu.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py /api/quote/generate: LLM call has no explicit timeout — if upstream stalls, the request will hang until client/proxy timeout. Consider asyncio.wait_for(chat.send_message(...), timeout=60).

server.py: EmailStr is lowercased on lookup ('entry.email.lower()') but stored via Pydantic which preserves casing on duplicate-return path. Persisted record uses lowercase consistently — OK, just be aware existing entries created via other paths could bypass dedup.

Estimator.jsx + Waitlist.jsx: both define `const API = ${process.env.REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL}/api` at module scope — fine, but no fallback/error if env var is undefined at build time. Acceptable for this MVP.
Frontend has no client-side validation on estimator description (>=10 chars) — backend returns 422 with detail and toast shows it; minor UX polish opportunity.
seed_data_creation: Tests create 1 waitlist entry per run with random ui_test_<ts>@example.com / test_<uuid>@example.com emails and 1 quote per run in db.quotes. Verified: db.quotes=2, db.waitlist=2 after run.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend test file at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py is pytest-based and reusable. AI quote endpoint takes 10-20s so AI_TIMEOUT=90 fixture is used. Public URL comes from /app/frontend/.env REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL. The 'Total Range' inner_text check in the Playwright script returned False on a re-run but the quote-readout DOM and screenshots confirm it renders correctly — treat as a script-timing false negative, not a bug.
